coredata.dc:title: Preparation a novel pyrene-based AIE-active ratiometric turn-on fluorescent probe for highly selective and sensitive detection of Hg2+
coredata.dc:creator: Ma, Jie
Xiao, Yao
Zhang, Cuihong
Zhang, Manman
Wang, Qingyang
Zheng, Weiguo
Zhang, Shuping
coredata.dc:description: Pyren-DT, as an AIE material, is facile prepared for detecting Hg2+ in aqueous. The obtained Pyren-DT sample is characterized via LC-MS and 1HNMR. Pyren-DT shows admirable an aggregation induced fluorescent emission (AIE) character in DMF-H2O mixture solvent with the water volume fraction (fw) beyond 80%. With the fw given at 70%, Pyren-DT probe presents turn-on fluorescent probe property with fluorescent intensity at 462 nm reliably positive linear with concentration of Hg2+. The LOD of the probe reaches 2.88 × 10−8 mol·L−1. The Pyren-DT probe shows good selectivity and sensibility towards Hg2+ and strong anti-interference towards some cations and anions. Pyrene-1-carbaldehyde is produced by the reaction between Pyren-DT and Hg2+. In the detection of real water samples, the probe shows better fluorescence characteristics in the system of river water samples rather than in the tap water samples for detecting mercury ion.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Fluorescent probe
2-(pyren-4-yl)-1,3-dithiolane
Mercury (II) ion
Mercaptal
Pyrene-1-carbaldehyde
The aggregation induced emission (AIE)

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Fluorescence emission changes of Pyren-DT towards Hg2+
Selectivity and anti-interference ability of Pyren-DT.
Contact mode detection between sensor Pyren-DT and Hg2+
Performance of Pyren-DT in detecting Hg2+ in real water specimens
The as-prepared Pyren-DT probe compared with some reported probes

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The fluorescence intensities of Pyren-DT (5 × 10−6 mol·L−1) change with H2O volume fraction of DMF-water system and the concentration of Hg2+. The excited light is given at 367 nm.
The changes of the fluorescence intensity of the probe upon the gradual addition of Hg2+ at 462 nm.
Digital photographs of Pyren-DT (5 × 10−6 mol·L−1) probe upon addition of different metal ions (2 eq) with a UV lamp (λex = 365 nm).

The fluorescence intensity of Pyren-DT (5 × 10−6 mol·L−1) probe upon various metal ions (the concentration of everyone set at 1 × 10−5 mol·L−1) in the absence or presence of Hg2+ (5 × 10−6 mol·L−1) at 462 nm.
Fluorescent intensity of Pyren-DT probe in the presence of 2 eq of different anions.
Photographs showing the color changes of sensor Pyren-DT before and after addition of Hg2+ under (a) sunlight and (b) 365 nm UV light.

(a) Fluorescence intensities of Pyren-DT with Hg2+ in tap water sample, (b) Fluorescence intensities of Pyren-DT with Hg2+ in deionized water sample; (c) Fluorescence intensities of Pyren-DT with Hg2+ in river water sample;(d) The fluorescent intensity of Pyren-DT probe at 462 nm with concentrations of Hg2+ in different water samples.

A. The fluorescence intensity of Pyren-DT probe changed with two given concentration of Hg2+, and Pyrene-1-CHO (5 × 10−6 mol·L−1) in DMF-H2O mixtures solvent; B. The Jablonski diagram of the fluorescent emission mechanism with two simulated electron energy states.
The radius of hydrated particles of Pyren-DT upon addition of different Hg2+ and Pyrene-1-CHO in DMF-water solvent.
The synthetic reaction of the Pyren-DT.
Schematic illustration of the reaction between Pyren-DT and Hg2+ ions.
The comparison with some reported Hg 2+ probes.

head.ce:title: Preparation a novel pyrene-based AIE-active ratiometric turn-on fluorescent probe for highly selective and sensitive detection of Hg2+

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Science, University of Shanghai for Science and Technology, Shanghai 200093, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: University of Shanghai for Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Science, University of Shanghai for Science and Technology, Shanghai 200093, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemistry, University of Kenturky, KY 40506, USA
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Chemistry, University of Kenturky, KY 40506, USA

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author at: College of Science, University of Shanghai for Science and Technology, Shanghai 200093, PR China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: University of Shanghai for Science and Technology

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Pyren-DT, as an AIE material, is facile prepared for detecting Hg2+ in aqueous. The obtained Pyren-DT sample is characterized via LC-MS and 1HNMR. Pyren-DT shows admirable an aggregation induced fluorescent emission (AIE) character in DMF-H2O mixture solvent with the water volume fraction (fw) beyond 80%. With the fw given at 70%, Pyren-DT probe presents turn-on fluorescent probe property with fluorescent intensity at 462 nm reliably positive linear with concentration of Hg2+. The LOD of the probe reaches 2.88 × 10−8 mol·L−1. The Pyren-DT probe shows good selectivity and sensibility towards Hg2+ and strong anti-interference towards some cations and anions. Pyrene-1-carbaldehyde is produced by the reaction between Pyren-DT and Hg2+. In the detection of real water samples, the probe shows better fluorescence characteristics in the system of river water samples rather than in the tap water samples for detecting mercury ion.

ce:section.ce:para: The molecular recognition and sensing systems for ions have been extensive attention [1]. Among the various ions, mercury is known as one of the most toxic and environmental pernicious metals. The traditional detection methods for mercury ion include atom absorption or atom emission spectroscopy [2,3], inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS) [4] and gas chromatography [5] and so on. However, these methods are not expediently applied in-site or on-line analysis situation due to they often need sophisticated analytical instrumentation, well-trained professionals and complex sample pre-treatment processes. In order to solve these problems, researchers have been exploiting and constructing various convenient sensors, such as the colorimetric [6], electrochemical [7], and fluorescence sensor [8–10]. The fluorescent probe converts the interaction between molecules and molecules into a fluorescent signal and transmits it to the outside world, which is helpful for us to understand the state, properties and changing rules of the micro-world.[11]

According to the mechanism of interaction between ion and probe molecule, the common fluorescent probes can be divided into a coordination type [12] and a reactions type [13]. Reactive fluorescent probes are widely concerned for their sensitivity and specificity. The thional or thionone structure can be applied to detect mercury ion in a suitable medium because the ion is used as a desulfurization reagent of them [14] Reactive fluorescent molecular probes have been designed and synthesized by introducing thiolaldehyde structure into fluorescent groups, which can be used as a ratiometric fluorescent molecular probe for mercury ion. We also are interested in the special reaction type and apply the thiolaldehyde structure for designing the fluorescent probe for mercury ion.

Since 2011, an aggregation induced emission (AIE) fluorescence phenomena have been paid more and more attentions and been a hot topic in the fluorescence field. Different from commonly used fluorescence molecules, which show a concentration quenching effect, these kinds of molecules with AIE mechanism have no or weak fluorescence emission in solution state, but show strong fluorescence emission in aggregation or solid state. [14] Based on AIE fluorescence mechanism, many kinds of molecules have been constructed and their fluorescences have been exploited, including tetraphenylethylene derivatives [15,16], tetraphenylsilane derivatives [17], and so on. These kinds of molecules exhibit many advantages on the strong intrinsic emission in aggregated state, good photo-bleaching resistance and high sensitivity as fluorescent probes. Among them, some AIE-active fluorescent probes for Hg 2+ have been prepared with exciting results [18–20]. However, many of them have some ineluctable disadvantages upon the complex structure, the difficult synthesis procedures, the high cost, and inacceptable detection limit. Therefore, it is still a significant challenge to design more efficient and accessible AIE-active fluorescent probe for detecting trace mercury ion in various mediums.

Pyrene fluorescent probes have been widely used in various researches in recent years. For example, by identifying changes in the microenvironment to affect the field of biological activity [21], including the detection of ambient environmental parameters such as temperature, viscosity, pressure, pH [22], and concentration. The detection of the distance between erbium probes was studied by the fluorescence emission spectrum of erbium-based activators [23]. In addition, it is also widely used in metal ion systems [24], anion systems, polymerization of thiols, peptides [25], critical micelle systems [26], and other biological systems. At the same time, erbium probes have become the most studied small organic molecules [27] in the field of optics. Pyrene has attracted particular interest because of high photoluminescence efficiency, high charge carrier mobility and excellent thermal stability. In addition, pyrene-1-carbaldehyde (Pyrene-1-CHO) molecule has also been an AIE-active fluorescence feature, it shows advantage on a relatively cheap, simple structural, and its emission wavelength adjusted via some given reactions. Despite these advantages, there is almost no research on using it and its derivatives as fluorescent probes. The mercaptan or thioketal reactions are a kind of commonly protective reaction for aldehyde or ketone groups in turn. Meanwhile, mercury ions are commonly used for a deprotection reaction from mercaptal / thioketal to aldehyde / ketone. Based on the above, we designed and synthesized a novel pyrene- based molecule (Pyren-DT) via a facile mercaptal reaction between pyrene-1-carbaldehyde and dithioglycol. The Pyren-DT presents good AIE-active in the given N, N-dimethyl formamide-water (DMF-H2O) mixed system. The Pyren-DT materials shows a “turn-on” type fluorescence sensor property with mercury ion at 462 nm. The Pyren-DT sensor has excellent selectivity, which is hardly interfered with many common metal ions and anions. Meanwhile, the probe demonstrates admirable sensitivity with suitable responding time, and the detection procedure can be completed in a few seconds. In this work, some significant experimental phenomena and results are investigated about Pyren-DT probe for mercury ion. It may be a threshold to prepare more similar pyrene-based AIE-type sensors for ion detection.

Pyrene-1-carbaldehyde, 1,2-ethanedithiol and iodine were purchased from the Energy Chemical (China), all other reagents and solvents were bought from the chemical reagents Co., Ltd. of Sinopharm Group (China). All the reagents were of analytical grade and used without further purification

NMR spectra were recorded by a Bruker AV-400 NMR spectrometer (Germany). The fluorescence intensities were monitored using a Hitachi F-7000 FL 220–240 V (Japan). LC-MS spectra were measured by a LCMS-2020 Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometer from Shimadzu Co. (Japan). ZF-I type three-purpose ultraviolet analyzer was bought from a Shanghai Analytical Instrument Co. of Sinopharm Group (China).

The Pyren-DT is synthesized via a representative one step mercaptal reaction shown in Scheme 1 . Pyrene-1-CHO (0.80 g, 3.27 mmol) and 1,2-ethanedithiol (0.33 mL, 3.92 mmol) were dissolved in trichloromethane (25 mL), and then a spot of iodine was added. When the mixture was stirred by a magnetic stirrer about 15 mins at room temperature, the reaction was quenched by the addition of Na2S2O3 (0.1 mol·L−1, 25 mL) and NaOH (10%, 25 mL). The as-obtained mixture solution was extracted with 30 mL chloroform for three times before the organic phase was separated.After then, the trichloromethane solvent was removed by a rotary evaporation instrument. The crude product was purified by a column chromatography with CH2Cl2/n-hexane (v/v = 1:3) as an eluent, and a pure light yellow solid the Pyren-DT was obtained (1.06 g, 94.64%) [28–33]. 1H NMR (CDCl3, 400 MHz, ppm): δ3.52–3.59(m, 2H), 3.64–3.71(m, 2H), 6.82(s, 1H), 8.01–8.08(m, 3H), 8.17–8.23(m, 4H), 8.21(d, J = 2.4, 1H), 8.23(d, J = 2.4, 1H).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence emission changes of Pyren-DT towards Hg2+
ce:section.ce:para: Pyren-DT can be dissoluble into DMF and insoluble into H2O solvent. So the solution of Pyren-DT in DMF was prepared, and then the solution is added into water dropwise for forming a suitable fluorescence mixture system. After repeated sifted, some typical fluorescence in various water volume percentage (fw) changed with concentration of Hg2+ are shown in Fig. 1 , the volume percentage of H2O in the DMF-H2O systems are set as 99%, 90%, 80%, 70%, 60% and 50% and their image of fluorescent changed with mercury ion are listed from Fig. 1a to 1f in turn.

The Pren-DT has fluorescence in theory, as the concentration of Hg2+ increases, the quantity of Pyren-DF gradually decreases, and the fluorescence intensity decreases linearly with the addition of Hg2+. At the same time, the fluorescence intensity of Pyrene-1-CHO produced by Pyren-DT reacting with Hg2+ increases linearly with the increase of Hg2+. However, the peak positions of Pyren-DT and Pyrene-1-CHO are so close that they cannot be completely separated due to the interference between the two peaks. Therefore, the fluorescence from Pyren-DT molecule should be eliminated for removing the interference to ion detection.

As can be seen from Fig. 1a to Fig. 1c, when the fw reduces for 99% to 80% in DMF-H2O system, the fluorescence of Pyren-DT molecule is fade way. This result further exhibits that Pyren-DT molecule presents the aggregation induced emission mechanism character. It is noticed that, the fluorescent from Pyren-DT molecule is completely disappeared, and the fluorescent intension of the system is linearly increased with the Hg2+ concentration shown in Fig. 2 when the fw is decreased to 70%. So that, it is proper that the water volume percentage (fw) is given at 70% in the sensor system constructed by Pyren-DT concentration at 5 × 10−6 mol·L−1 in DMF-H2O mixed solvent. As anticipated, the fluorescence intensity at 462 nm was linearly increased with the concentration of Hg2+ in the range from 5 × 10−7 mol∙L−1 to 5 × 10−6 mol∙L−1 (R2 = 0.9986, Fig. 2), it is consistent with a typical turn-on sensor property. According to the equation 3σ/S, the detection limit was calculated to below 2.88 × 10−8 mol·L−1, which suggests that the Pyren-DT sensor system has preeminent detection stability and reliability. This change of fluorescence intensity is linked to the amount-of-substance concentration of Pyren-DT, which changed with the reaction progresses based on aggregation induced emission (AIE) mechanism. So Pyren-DT can be used as a potential ratio-metric fluorescent probe for Hg2+ detection.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity and anti-interference ability of Pyren-DT.
ce:section.ce:para: Selectivity and anti-interference are significant indicators for measuring a probe. Pyren-DT shows good selectivity fluorescence properties toward Hg2+ over other metal ions (Mn+) proved by some real images in Fig. 3 . When Hg2+ is added to Pyren-DT probe system, the colour of the probe obviously changes with a UV lamp (λ = 365 nm) excitation. On the contrary, although the concentration of each ion is set to twice the concentration of mercury and Pyren-DT, none of the teens common ions examined can make the Pyren-DT sensor system produce fluorescence, including Ca2+, Bi3+, Mg2+, Sn2+, K+, Co2+, Ni2+, Sr2+, Zn2+, Cu2+, Fe2+, Fe3+, and Pb2+, respectively. The competition experiment also is executed by adding Hg2+ to the solution of Pyren-DT for DMF-water in the presence of other metal ions at 462 nm. The result is presented in Fig. 4 . As is expected, the probe with mixed Mn+ and Hg2+ only shows similar fluorescence intensity with single Hg2+ ion effect. Among them, some ions, including Ca2+, Sr2+, Zn2+, and Pb2+ ion, can make the fluorescent intensity slight reduce, others make the intensity increase a bit. This result suggests the Pyren-DT possess excellent selectivity toward Hg2+ ion and can be applied to qualitatively and semi-quantitatively detect the ion.

In addition, the effects of some anions have also been investigated, various anions including NO3 −, CO3 2−, SO4 2−, CH3COO− and HCO3 − are examined in parallel under the same condition, and comparative results are shown in Fig. 5 . The experimental results indicate that the anions do not almost influence the fluorescence intensity of the detecting system. Meanwhile, it is not found that the emission peak at 462 nm with anions presence. Above results show that the as-prepared Pyren-DT can be excellent selective and sensitive fluorescence sensor for Hg2+ ion in DMF-H2O mixture solvent.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Contact mode detection between sensor Pyren-DT and Hg2+
ce:section.ce:para: The recognition of sensor Pyren-DT toward Hg2+ is tested on pre-treated filter paper to explore its versatility application as a simple and efficient probe. The test strips are prepared by immersing the filter paper into the solution of sensor Pyren-DT in DMF for a few seconds, the concentration of Pyren-DT is 1.0 × 10−3 mol·L−1. Then, these strips dried in air and subsequently treated with Hg2+ solution (1.0 × 10−3 mol·L−1) in water for solid state experiments. The filter paper soaked with Pyren-DT (1.0 × 10−3 mol·L−1, DMF as solution) shows white and wathet in sunlight and the 365 nm UV light in turn (Fig. 6 ). However, when the Pyren-DT soaking filter is dropped some Hg2+–H2O solution (1.0 × 10−3 mol L−1), the color of the filter changes from white under sunlight to navy-blue under UV light (365 nm). The result suggests the as-prepared Pyren-DT molecule may offer a convenient and cost-effective strategy for facile detection of Hg2+ ion.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Performance of Pyren-DT in detecting Hg2+ in real water specimens
ce:section.ce:para: In order to verify whether Pyren-DT can also perform stably in actual water environment, tap water pure water and actual river water specimens were separately prepared. Except a river water samples and a tap water samples (from Shanghai) are replaced distilled water for prepare Pyren-DT probe solutions, all measured condition and operations are same with the probe shown in Fig. 1. From Fig. 7 , it shows that the fluorescence intensity of probe also shows upward trends with the increase concentrations of Hg2+ ion, despite the interference of many minerals and organic matters in the natural water. Compared Fig. 7a to c, it is found that the fluorescent intensity of probe in lake water sample is obviously higher than in pure water and higher than tap water, under the same concentration of Hg2+. It can be clearly seen from Fig. 7d that at 462 nm, there are differences in the fluorescence intensities of tap water, pure water and river water, but all of them have an increasing trend. The fluorescent intensity of the probe reaches 2500 au in the lake water sample, meanwhile it only reaches 93 au in the tap water. The significant difference between the three groups of water samples may be attributed to the presence of strong oxidizing substance residues in tap water samples, which led to the destruction of fluorescent substances. It suggests the as-prepared Pyren-DT probe is more applicable to detect Hg2+ in the lake water sample due to higher fluorescent responding.

The fluorescent of the Pyren-DT probe is not detected when the volume percentage of H2O is given at 30% in the DMF-H2O system, as showed in Fig. 8 A. Meanwhile, when the Hg2+ of 0.5 eq and 1 eq is added into the system, the fluorescence intensity is getting more and more stronger. Although the fluorescence intensity is still less than that of Pyren-1-carbaldehyde, the position of fluorescence emission peak is the same. This probe was designed according to the Hg2+-promoted deprotection reaction of mercaptal, transforming Pyren-DT to Pyrene-1-CHO in the presence of Hg2+. To further confirm this conjecture, fluorescence spectra of Pyrene-1-CHO, Pyren-DT and typical Hg2+- Pyren-DT mixture are obtained and shown in Fig. 8A.

Compared to Pyren-DT with Pyrene-1-CHO, the fluorescence intensity is increase, which is a typical “turn-on” fluorescence probe, in the same DMF-H2O mixtures solvent. It indicates that the fluorescence of pyrene- carboxaldehyde-based molecule can be controlled by acetal reaction [34]. With Hg2+ ion is added from 0 to 1 equivalent. The maximum fluorescence intension of the detecting system is obtained with emission wavelength at 462 nm, which is in accordance with the result obtained via the system made up of Pyrene-1-CHO in DMF-H2O mixtures. According to the molecular orbital theory and the density functional theory, the UV–vis absorbing spectrum and fluorescent spectrum are simulated, and the Jablonski diagram of the fluorescent mechanism is shown in Fig. 8B. The ground state electron (HOMO) is excited by 367 nm light and transfers to the excited state (LUMO), then the electron releases 460 nm fluorescence and returns to ground state. Meanwhile, the calculated the largest position of UV–vis absorbing peak and fluorescent peak of Pyrene-1-CHO in DMF solution is about 370 nm and 453 nm in turn. Because it mainly matches the experimental result, the fluorescent mechanism should be attributed to Pyrene-1-CHO produced via the reaction between Pyren-DT and Hg2+ ion. When Hg2+ ion is the molar equivalent with Pyren-DT molecule, the fluorescence intension of 462 nm almost enhances to the maximum. It suggests that the reacting ratio might be 1:1 (mol/mol) between compounds as showed in Scheme 2 . Pyren-DT is severed by Hg2+ ions on the basis of Hg2+-promoted deprotection of thioacetal[35].

For further confirming the presume, we detect the radius of hydration particles about Pyren-DT and Pyrene-1-CHO in the DMF-H2O (fw 70%). The experimental results show that the radius of hydration molecule of Pyrene-1-CHO is larger than that of Pyren-DT. In Pyren-DT DMF-H2O dispersion system, with the addition of Hg2+, the radius of the system tends to the radius of Pyrene-1-CHO system. Because Pyren-DT is less polar than Pyrene-1-CHO. In addition, the aldehyde group also contains hydrogen bonds, which make Pyrene-1-CHO bind to water more closely than Pyren-DT in DMF-H2O system. So, with the addition of Hg2+, the radius of the system increases gradually. According to the graph shown in Fig. 9 , this phenomenon is consistent with the mechanism of aggregation induced emission. Therefore, it has been demonstrated that we have designed and synthesized a novel mercury ion fluorescent probe.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The as-prepared Pyren-DT probe compared with some reported probes
ce:section.ce:para: Compared with some reported mercury ion AIE-active fluorescent probes, the as-synthesized probe shows some advantages upon facile synthesis, highly sensitive reaction, good selectivity and prominent low detection limit. As shown in Table 1 , the operation environment is strict and complicated of the first two probes [36,37]. The third and fourth because of the complex structure, tedious synthesis process and high cost restrain their application. Meanwhile, the concentration of mercury ions shows poor linearity relationship with the fluorescence intension [38,39]. Compare with AA-AR probe [40], the as-prepared Pyren-DT probe presents some advantages not only on facile one-step synthesized reaction with high yield, but on less LOD for detecting mercury ion, which is more benefit to application of Pyren-DT probe.

In summary, we have successfully designed and synthesized a novel fluorescent probe Pyren-DT based on Pyrene-1-CHO and ethanedithiol. The probe presents many advantages on sensitive and selective detection of Hg2+ in DMF-water mixtures solvent with fw given at 70%. It demonstrates excellent linear relationships between fluorescent intensity and Hg2+ concentrations in the range of 0–6.5 μmol∙L−1 and the detection limit reaches 2.88 × 10−8mol·L−1. The probe is not influenced by teens kinds of metal ions and anions. Pyren-DT demonstrates fluorescent character based on aggregation induced emission (AIE) mechanism. The fluorescent mechanism of the probe is attributed to Pyrene-1-CHO produced by reaction between Pyren-DT and Hg2+ ion, which is accordance with the reaction-type fluorescence probe properties. The probe also presents reliable detection property in real water samples and filter sample. The further research will be on exploiting the application of the probe in bio-samples and more real water samples.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The authors are thankful to the natural science foundation of Shanghai (No.15ZR1428500) and the national natural science foundation of China (No. 20906061) and for financial supports.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mseb.2020.114582.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary data 1
